key_insight: A single-layer SiO2 hard mask plus multi-step oxygen ICP-RIE enables complete through-etching of 16 µm single-crystal diamond membranes, and the remaining oxide can be used directly for room-temperature bonding to silica.
engineering_relevance: This provides a scalable, contamination-free route to diamond-on-silica heterostructures for integrated quantum photonics and sensing without metal masks or high-temperature bonding.
method: High-selectivity O2-based ICP-RIE with a SiO2 hard mask to etch deep diamond features.
Room-temperature bonding after O2 plasma activation using a sodium silicate interlayer.
Use of the residual oxide as the bonding surface for transfer to SiO2 substrates.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports complete through-etch of a 16 µm-thick NV-doped diamond membrane, ~15:1 diamond-to-SiO2 selectivity, preserved sidewall/surface quality, and no detectable parasitic photoluminescence after bonding.
watch_item: Process scalability may depend on tightly controlling plasma etch uniformity and interlayer bonding quality to avoid membrane damage or variability in optical performance.
paper_title: Scalable Fabrication of Diamond-on-Silica Heterostructures via High-Selectivity Deep ICP-RIE and Room-Temperature Bonding
